summary: Level-Up HP Storage, Multiclass Modal, and Multiclass API Testing - 27 tests (10 backend + 17 frontend). Backend tests verify hp_gained storage for all level types (standard, ASI, feat) and multiclass endpoints. Frontend tests verify LevelUpModal mode selection, regular level-up flow, and multiclass flow UI. FOUND BUG: Multiclass endpoint has class field mismatch.

critical.issue: Line 6433 uses character.get('class') but player_characters model uses 'character_class'. This causes original class to be stored as 'Unknown' instead of the actual class name (e.g., 'Fighter').
critical.fix: Change line 6433 from character.get('class', 'Unknown') to character.get('character_class', 'Unknown')

breakdown.test_multiclass_and_hp_gained.py (backen: 10/10 passed - HP gained storage and multiclass APIs

action_items: CRITICAL BUG FIX: In /app/backend/server.py line 6433, change character.get('class', 'Unknown') to character.get('character_class', 'Unknown') - this causes multiclass to store 'Unknown' as the original class instead of the actual class name
seed_data_creation: None - tests use existing test characters and create/cleanup test data
context_for_next_testing_agent: HP gained storage is now verified for all level types (standard, ASI, feat). LevelUpModal UI tests cover mode selection, regular level-up flow, and multiclass class selection. The multiclass backend has a critical bug (line 6433) that stores 'Unknown' as the original class. Tests document this bug and will fail when fixed (update assertions from 'unknown' to the actual class name after fix).

hp_gained_storage.details: Verified hp_gained is stored in level_progression for standard, ASI, and feat level-ups. Also verified average HP calculation.

multiclass_api.bug: Original class stored as 'Unknown' due to wrong field name (class vs character_class)

rca_of_issue.root_cause: server.py line 6433 uses character.get('class') but player_characters model uses 'character_class' as the field name
rca_of_issue.reproduction: 1. Create a Fighter character, 2. Call POST /api/characters/{id}/multiclass with class_name='Cleric', 3. Check response - classes array shows 'Unknown' instead of 'Fighter'
